_comment: Universal dust threshold — the smallest amount of value the app will act on. Denominated in abstract 'units' of a reference asset (not USD) so the threshold doesn't silently loosen as fiat inflates: a USD-pegged guard would eventually cause dust-loop bugs as crypto/token prices rose with inflation. The live USD price of one unit is fetched from priceSourceTokens (first non-zero source wins; try each token via Moralis, then DexScreener). thresholdUsd = thresholdUnits × usdPerUnit. Default: 1/4800 ≈ $1 when one unit is worth $4800. Current reference asset is one troy ounce of gold, via PAXG (alpha) and XAUT (fallback) — both 1:1 redeemable for one oz physical gold. The code paths are generic; to switch reference assets, replace the tokens here (and keep thresholdUnits consistent with the new asset's price scale). 'chain' must match a key in src/price-fetcher.js _MORALIS_CHAINS; 'dexScreenerChain' is DexScreener's chain identifier. Consume via src/dust.js (DUST_THRESHOLD_UNITS export, isDust, getDustThresholdUsd).
